chapters.title: Greetings & Introductions
chapters.description: Learn how to say hello, goodbye, and introduce yourself in French.

lessons.explanation: 'Bonjour' is the standard greeting used during the day in French.
'Je m'appelle' means 'My name is' — literally 'I call myself'.
'Au revoir' literally means 'until we see again'.

lessons.hint: 'Enchanté' is the standard phrase when meeting someone for the first time.

learningMaterial.title: Greetings & Introductions
learningMaterial.intro: Learn how to say hello, goodbye, and introduce yourself in French.

concepts.explanation: 'Bonjour' is the standard greeting used during the day in French.
'Je m'appelle' means 'My name is' — literally 'I call myself'.
Use 'comment' (how) + 'tu vas' (you go/are).
'Au revoir' literally means 'until we see again'.
'Enchanté' is the standard phrase when meeting someone for the first time.

chapters.title: Numbers & Counting
chapters.description: Count from 1 to 20 and use numbers in everyday situations.

lessons.explanation: 'Cinq' = 5. Remember: un, deux, trois, quatre, cinq.

lessons.prompt: J'ai ___ ans. (I am ten years old.)
lessons.explanation: 'Dix' means ten. 'J'ai dix ans' = I am ten years old.
'Vingt' = 20. After twenty: vingt et un (21), vingt-deux (22).

lessons.prompt: Il y a ___ élèves dans la classe. (There are twelve students in the class.)
lessons.explanation: 'Douze' = 12. 'Il y a' means 'there are'.

learningMaterial.title: Numbers & Counting
learningMaterial.intro: Count from 1 to 20 and use numbers in everyday situations.

concepts.explanation: 'Cinq' = 5. Remember: un, deux, trois, quatre, cinq.
'Dix' means ten. 'J'ai dix ans' = I am ten years old.
Use 'j'ai' (I have) + 'deux' (two) + 'frères' (brothers).
'Vingt' = 20. After twenty: vingt et un (21), vingt-deux (22).
'Douze' = 12. 'Il y a' means 'there are'.

chapters.title: Colors & Descriptions
chapters.description: Learn colors and how to describe objects in French.

lessons.explanation: 'Rouge' means red. Adjectives in French agree with the noun's gender.

lessons.prompt: Le ciel est ___. (The sky is blue.)
lessons.explanation: 'Bleu' means blue. Since 'ciel' (sky) is masculine, we use 'bleu' not 'bleue'.
'Jaune' = yellow. It stays the same for both masculine and feminine nouns.

lessons.prompt: J'aime la couleur ___. (I like the color green.)
lessons.explanation: 'Vert' = green (masculine), 'verte' = green (feminine). 'Couleur' is feminine.

learningMaterial.title: Colors & Descriptions
learningMaterial.intro: Learn colors and how to describe objects in French.

concepts.explanation: 'Rouge' means red. Adjectives in French agree with the noun's gender.
'Bleu' means blue. Since 'ciel' (sky) is masculine, we use 'bleu' not 'bleue'.
'Maison' is feminine, so 'blanc' becomes 'blanche'.
'Jaune' = yellow. It stays the same for both masculine and feminine nouns.
'Vert' = green (masculine), 'verte' = green (feminine). 'Couleur' is feminine.

chapters.title: Family Members
chapters.description: Learn the names of family members and describe your family.

lessons.explanation: 'Mère' = mother, 'père' = father. La mère, le père.

lessons.prompt: Mon ___ s'appelle Paul. (My brother's name is Paul.)
lessons.explanation: 'Frère' = brother, 'sœur' = sister.
'Grand-père' = grandfather, 'grand-mère' = grandmother, 'grands-parents' = grandparents.

lessons.prompt: Ma ___ a quarante ans. (My aunt is forty years old.)
lessons.explanation: 'Tante' = aunt, 'oncle' = uncle.

learningMaterial.title: Family Members
learningMaterial.intro: Learn the names of family members and describe your family.

concepts.explanation: 'Mère' = mother, 'père' = father. La mère, le père.
'Frère' = brother, 'sœur' = sister.
'Grande' means big/large. 'Famille' is feminine.
'Grand-père' = grandfather, 'grand-mère' = grandmother, 'grands-parents' = grandparents.
'Tante' = aunt, 'oncle' = uncle.

chapters.title: Food & Drinks
chapters.description: Order food and drinks and talk about what you like to eat.

lessons.explanation: 'Eau' = water. 'Un verre d'eau' = a glass of water.

lessons.prompt: Je voudrais du ___. (I would like some bread.)
lessons.explanation: 'Pain' = bread. 'Je voudrais' = I would like (polite way to order).

lessons.hint: Use 'j'aime' (I like) + 'le fromage' (cheese, with definite article).

lessons.explanation: 'Petit-déjeuner' = breakfast, 'déjeuner' = lunch, 'dîner' = dinner.
'S'il vous plaît' = please (formal). Essential phrase at a restaurant.

learningMaterial.title: Food & Drinks
learningMaterial.intro: Order food and drinks and talk about what you like to eat.

concepts.explanation: 'Eau' = water. 'Un verre d'eau' = a glass of water.
'Pain' = bread. 'Je voudrais' = I would like (polite way to order).
Use 'j'aime' (I like) + 'le fromage' (cheese, with definite article).
'Petit-déjeuner' = breakfast, 'déjeuner' = lunch, 'dîner' = dinner.
'S'il vous plaît' = please (formal). Essential phrase at a restaurant.

chapters.title: Days, Months & Time
chapters.description: Talk about days of the week, months, and tell the time in French.

lessons.explanation: Days in French: lundi, mardi, mercredi, jeudi, vendredi, samedi, dimanche.

lessons.prompt: Aujourd'hui, c'est le ___ janvier. (Today is the first of January.)
lessons.explanation: For the first of the month, use 'premier'. For other days, use the number: le deux, le trois...
Months: janvier, février, mars, avril, mai, juin, juillet, août, septembre, octobre, novembre, décembre.

lessons.prompt: Il est ___ heures. (It is three o'clock.)
lessons.explanation: 'Il est trois heures' = It is 3 o'clock. For noon: 'il est midi', for midnight: 'il est minuit'.

learningMaterial.title: Days, Months & Time
learningMaterial.intro: Talk about days of the week, months, and tell the time in French.

concepts.explanation: Days in French: lundi, mardi, mercredi, jeudi, vendredi, samedi, dimanche.
For the first of the month, use 'premier'. For other days, use the number: le deux, le trois...
'Quelle heure' = what time, 'est-il' = is it.
Months: janvier, février, mars, avril, mai, juin, juillet, août, septembre, octobre, novembre, décembre.
'Il est trois heures' = It is 3 o'clock. For noon: 'il est midi', for midnight: 'il est minuit'.
